evals.prompt: Use $orchestrated-delivery to execute the existing package at docs/implementation/epics/E7/execution. It is marked ready_for_implementation. Stop after local commits unless I explicitly ask for PR publication.
evals.expected_output: The agent validates the package, binds runtime/provider facts, presents or follows an approved execution plan, dispatches packaged implementer and reviewer prompts in dependency waves, commits approved pathsets, creates tracker evidence commits, and stops before PR publication.
assertions: OD-1: The run starts only after finding an existing package marked ready_for_implementation.
OD-2: The agent records runtime/provider facts without editing package-owned scope, prompts, ACs, dependency order, model class, or effort.
OD-3: Dependency waves launch only when prerequisite story and tracker evidence commits exist.
OD-4: Each story reuses one implementer and one reviewer context, and workers do not stage, commit, push, PR, merge, or close contexts.
OD-5: Coordinator treats reviewer approval as advisory and commits only the approved pathset after its own scope/gate inspection.
OD-6: Each completed story has a story commit followed by a tracker-only evidence commit.
OD-7: The agent stops at the requested local-commit boundary.
OD-8: Updates are sparse and evidence-based.

evals.prompt: Use $orchestrated-delivery on this execution folder. It has plan.md and tracker.md, but no package-contained deep readiness verdict and one implementer prompt says to infer missing ACs from the code.
evals.expected_output: The agent refuses execution and routes the package back to plan-delivery or plan-epic as appropriate. It does not repair prompts, invent ACs, reorder stories, change model class or effort, or dispatch workers.
assertions: OD-1: Missing deep readiness evidence or incomplete prompts cause refusal.
OD-1: Structural file presence alone is not treated as readiness.
OD-2: The agent does not author or repair package-owned decisions.
OD-8: The refusal is concise and names the owning planning repair path.

evals.prompt: A worker agent reports that it staged its own changes and created a commit before handing back to the coordinator.
evals.expected_output: The skill treats the worker's staging and commit as a forbidden violation. The coordinator owns all staging, commit, push, PR, and merge actions. The coordinator re-addresses or rejects the worker output and does not proceed with the worker-authored commit.
assertions: OD-4: Workers never stage, commit, push, PR, merge, close contexts, or mark stories complete.
OD-4: Only the coordinator performs staging, commit, push, PR creation, merge, and closure.
OD-4: The coordinator re-addresses or rejects a worker that reported self-mutation; the worker-authored commit is not accepted as-is.
OD-5: Coordinator independently inspects diff and gate before any commit regardless of worker claims.

evals.prompt: During $orchestrated-delivery, reviewer R1 returns blocking findings for story S1. The coordinator starts a new implementer I2 with the findings and then starts a new reviewer R2 to rereview the fix, because the copied context should be enough.
evals.expected_output: The skill rejects the replacement-worker loop. The coordinator must message the existing implementer context with the exact findings, then message the existing reviewer context with the latest diff/evidence for rereview. Replacement is allowed only after a recorded lost or unusable context exception and explicit coordinator action.
assertions: OD-4: A story keeps exactly one implementer context and one reviewer context across fix/rereview rounds.
OD-4: Reviewer findings are sent to the existing implementer context rather than a fresh implementer.
OD-4: The fix is sent to the existing reviewer context for rereview rather than a fresh reviewer.
OD-4: Replacement requires a recorded lost/unusable-context exception before any new worker is created.
OD-5: The coordinator still inspects the latest diff and gate evidence before rereview or commit.

evals.prompt: An implementer stops on story core-02-s2 and reports a source-contract blocker: ACs require policy-deny and approved-parent scope decisions, but the package exposes only policyRef and no approved-parent source. No code changes were made.
evals.expected_output: The coordinator treats the report as a planning blocker, records the affected ACs and missing facts in the tracker blockers/notes, does not create a story commit, does not unlock dependents, and routes repair to plan-epic or plan-delivery as appropriate.
assertions: OD-9: Source-contract blockers are treated as planning blockers rather than implementer failures.
OD-9: The coordinator does not ask the worker to invent missing policy or scope semantics.
OD-9: The affected tracker row records status/blockers/notes with affected ACs, missing facts, worker alias, and route-back target.
OD-3: No dependent story is dispatched or unlocked because no valid story commit and tracker evidence commit exist.
OD-6: No story commit is created for a blocked story with no approved pathset.

evals.prompt: After all stories committed locally and a PR is open, a reviewer has approved the PR. The user has not issued any current merge instruction.
evals.expected_output: The skill refuses to merge, push, or perform any cleanup. It treats the PR review approval as detect-only, stops at the requested boundary, and waits for an explicit current merge instruction from the user before taking any further action.
assertions: OD-7: PR review waiting is detect-only; the skill does not merge without an explicit current user instruction.
OD-7: The skill does not push, merge, or clean up worker contexts after review approval absent a current instruction.
OD-7: The skill stops at the requested boundary and reports the PR state without acting on it.
OD-8: The closeout is sparse and evidence-based; no transcript or diff is dumped.

evals.prompt: Use $orchestrated-delivery on the ready package and open a PR when all stories land. Do not wait for review, merge, or clean up.
evals.expected_output: The agent completes local story and tracker commits, opens or updates the PR only after authorization, reports the PR URL, and stops without review waiting, merge, cleanup, or worker-context overreach.
assertions: OD-6: Story commits and tracker evidence commits exist before publication.
OD-7: PR publication respects the requested boundary and stops after reporting the URL.
OD-7: Review waiting is not started without explicit request.
OD-8: The closeout reports evidence rather than dumping transcripts or diffs.
